title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Following the discovery of a body in a sarcophagus at a warehouse, an unidentified man expresses distress over the death of a woman he compares to a piece of art from Naples. As Detective Bacon and Campbell walk toward the manor, two boys on bicycles, Alexander Eastrie and James Stottard West, plead to see the body. Bacon allows them to enter the warehouse via an officer named Sanders.

Bacon later summons Lucy Esperanza, the Crickenshaw family's maid, for questioning. Lucy explains that she went to the warehouse to find paint but instead used a crowbar to open the sarcophagus because she was looking for a body. She reveals she was hired for this task by Jane Marple, an elderly woman residing at 4 Madison Road. Lucy expresses concern that if Bacon tells her employer, Emma, that she was searching for a corpse, she might lose her job and be unable to support her family.

Bacon discusses the case with the county police chief, noting that Lucy is likely not a local. They plan to hold an inquest the following day, hoping members of the Crickenshaw family—including Harold, Alfred, and the painter Sederick—might identify the deceased. Bacon expresses skepticism regarding Jane Marple’s "fantastical" claims but acknowledges she successfully hired Lucy to find the body.

The inquest concludes that the cause of death was strangulation, though no suspects are identified. Following the proceedings, members of the Crickenshaw family, including Emma, Cecil, Harold, Brian Eastleigh, and the lawyer Mr. Wimborne, depart in a limousine. Alexander and James West, who had watched the inquest from the hall, approach the group.
